question: [File: 'File Name: src/SortedTroves.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: _remove (line 202)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS where NodeRemoved(_id) is called as a function (not emit NodeRemoved(_id)), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: in Solidity 0.6.11, calling an event as a function is a no-op — the event is not emitted; this means off-chain indexers and monitoring systems do not receive NodeRemoved events, violating INVARIANT that all list modifications must emit the corresponding events for off-chain consistency, causing scoped impact: off-chain systems (liquidation bots, redemption bots) that rely on NodeRemoved events to track list state will have stale data, potentially missing liquidation opportunities or attempting to liquidate already-closed troves, indirectly enabling insolvent troves to persist? Local proof idea: Foundry test: call _remove, assert NodeRemoved event is emitted (it won't be), document the missing emit.

[File: 'File Name: src/SortedTroves.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: _descendList / _ascendList] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS where the list has exactly 2 nodes (head H with NICR=200, tail T with NICR=100) and the attacker wants to insert a node with NICR=50 (should go after tail), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: _findInsertPosition with prevId=T, nextId=address(0); _descendList(_troveManager, 50, T); checks 'data.head == T' — false; enters while loop with prevId=T, nextId=data.nodes[T].nextId=address(0); _validInsertPosition(50, T, address(0)) checks 'data.tail == T && 50 <= getNominalICR(T)=100' — true; loop exits, returns (T, address(0)) — CORRECT; now test with NICR=250 (should go before head): _findInsertPosition with prevId=address(0), nextId=H; _ascendList(_troveManager, 250, H); checks 'data.tail == H' — false; enters while loop with nextId=H, prevId=data.nodes[H].prevId=address(0); _validInsertPosition(250, address(0), H) checks 'data.head == H && 250 >= getNominalICR(H)=200' — true; returns (address(0), H) — CORRECT; now test with NICR=150 (between H and T): _findInsertPosition with prevId=H, nextId=T; _validInsertPosition(150, H, T) checks 'data.nodes[H].nextId == T && getNominalICR(H)=200 >= 150 && 150 >= getNominalICR(T)=100' — true; returns (H, T) — CORRECT; so the 2-node case works correctly; now test the edge case where NICR exactly equals head or tail NICR, violating INVARIANT that equal-NICR troves can be inserted in arbitrary order, causing scoped impact: ordering manipulation among equal-NICR troves? Local proof idea: unit test all NICR values relative to a 2-node list.

[File: 'File Name: src/SortedTroves.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: insert / _insert] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS where the attacker opens a trove with NICR = type(uint256).max (maximum possible NICR, achieved with very high collateral and minimum debt), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) openTrove with msg.value = very large, _USDSAmount = minimum; (2) NICR = coll * 1e20 / debt — could overflow if coll is very large; (3) _insert is called with newNICR = overflowed value; (4) _validInsertPosition compares overflowed NICR with existing NICRs; (5) the trove might be inserted at the wrong position, violating INVARIANT that NICR computation must not overflow for any valid trove parameters, causing scoped impact: trove inserted at wrong position, affecting liquidation/redemption ordering? Local proof idea: Foundry fuzz test with extreme collateral and debt values, assert NICR computation does not overflow and the resulting position is correct.
